normalized_title: Planning Bulletin 113/2015: Multiple Dwellings in R40 Coded Areas and Variation to R-Codes Multiple Dwelling Development Standards
scope_summary: Guidance to WA local governments on acceptable local planning scheme amendments to restrict multiple dwelling development in R40 coded areas, by applying the average site area per grouped dwelling standard in lieu of plot ratio for R40 lots located outside defined core/activity-centre walkable catchments. It also outlines the WAPC-supported approach to local planning policies dealing with proposals seeking variation to R-Codes multiple dwelling deemed-to-comply standards, including maximum plot ratio bonuses and the qualitative criteria that should be achieved.

key_numeric_standards.topic: core area catchment - activity centre / rail station
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: R40 lots within 800 m of a strategic metropolitan, secondary, district or specialised activity centre or high-frequency rail station are excluded from multiple-dwelling restriction

key_numeric_standards.topic: optional locational criterion - public open space
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: proximity to public open space (at local government discretion)

key_numeric_standards.topic: optional locational criterion - local/neighbourhood activity centre
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: proximity to a local or neighbourhood activity centre (at local government discretion)

key_numeric_standards.topic: optional locational criterion - high frequency bus stop
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: proximity to a bus stop on a high frequency bus route (at local government discretion)
multiple dwellings in R30 to R80 coded areas
multiple dwellings in R100 and greater coded areas

cross_references.cited: Model Scheme Text (MST clauses 5.3 and 5.5, Part 2)

quality_flags: Bulletin is grounded in the pre-2015 framework (Model Scheme Text clauses, R-Codes 2015 amendments, R-Codes clause 6.1.1/Table 4 plot ratio); the R-Codes Vol.1 2024 removed plot ratio control for R40-R60 multiple dwellings in favour of average site area per dwelling (see PB 114/2024), so this bulletin's operative weight today is doubtful even though it remains the latest published version
Multi-column PDF interleaving scrambles sentence order in places
summary.json key_provisions is empty despite extractable section content
